apiOrSchemaChanges: + export function getAttrDef(tagName: string, attrName: string): SsmlAttrDef | undefined
failingTest: No existing failing test; the work adds a new function and its tests.
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — add the getAttrDef function and export it
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — import getAttrDef, add four test cases covering known attribute, case-insensitive match, unknown tag, and known tag with unknown attribute
fixApproach: Add a new exported function `getAttrDef(tagName, attrName)` to tags.ts that first delegates to the existing `getTagDef` for case-insensitive tag resolution, then does a case-insensitive `.find` on the tag's `attributes` array by `a.name`. Returns the matching `SsmlAttrDef` or `undefined`. No caching or other modules touched.
steps: 1. In `apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts`, add the `getAttrDef` function after `getTagDef`: call `getTagDef(tagName)`, if falsy return `undefined`, otherwise `def.attributes.find((a) => a.name.toLowerCase() === attrName.toLowerCase())` and return the result (or `undefined`). Ensure it is exported.
2. In `apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts`, add `getAttrDef` to the existing import from `./tags`.

3. In the same test file, inside the existing top-level `describe('SSML Tag Registry', ...)`, add a new nested `describe('getAttrDef', ...)` block with four it-cases: (a) known attribute (`break`/`time`) returns the correct `SsmlAttrDef`, (b) case-insensitive match (`BREAK`/`TIME`) returns the same result, (c) unknown tag returns `undefined`, (d) known tag with unknown attribute returns `undefined`.
4. Run `npm run test` from the repo root and confirm all tests pass.
summary: Add getAttrDef(tagName, attrName) case-insensitive attribute lookup to the SSML tag registry with four vitest test cases.
